name: Braised Lamb Shanks with Roasted Garlic & White Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dried Cannellini beans, great northern or navy beans
4 lamb shanks, trimmed of fat and membrane (1 pound)
2 tablespoons fresh rosemary or 2 teaspoons dried rosemary
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort beans, discarding any debris. Rinse and place in a large bowl.
2. Cover with cold water and let soak for at least 8 hours (overnight).
3. Alternatively, cover beans with water in a large pot and bring to a boil.
4. Boil for 2 minutes. Remove from the heat and let stand for 1 hour.
5. Preheat oven to 300 degrees. Remove as much of the outer husk of garlic as possible without separating cloves. Wrap loosely in aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
6. Unwrap foil, separate cloves slightly so they cook evenly. Wrap again and bake for 30 minutes longer, or until garlic is very soft. Set aside to cool.
7. Increase oven temperature to 325 degrees.
8. In a Dutch oven, heat 1 1/2 teaspoons olive oil over medium-high heat. Add lamb shanks and brown on all sides. Remove from pan and drain on paper towels.
9. Add remaining 1 1/2 teaspoons oil to the pan, then add carrots, onions, and celery; sauté for 2 to 3 minutes, or until softened. Add wine and cook until reduced by half (5-7 minutes). Stir in beef stock, tomatoes, rosemary, and bay leaf.
10. Peel half of roasted garlic cloves and add to the pan. Bring to a boil. Return meat to the pan, cover tightly, and place in oven. Bake for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, turning shanks occasionally, or until meat is very tender.
11. Meanwhile, drain beans and place in a large heavy pot. Add water to cover by about 4 inches and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until beans are tender. Drain.
12. Remove bay leaf from sauce and skim off fat. Boil the sauce for about 5 minutes, or until slightly thickened. Peel remaining roasted garlic and add to sauce along with drained beans. Heat through and season with salt and pepper.
13. Mound bean mixture on a platter and place lamb shanks on top. Serves 4.
CALORIES: 445 PER SERVING; PROTEIN: 40 GRAMS; FAT: 10 GRAMS; CARBOHYDRATE: 46 GRAMS; SODIUM: 518 MILLIGRAMS; CHOLESTEROL: 89 MILLIGRAMS.
